summary: Disables Delivery Optimization peer-to-peer update sharing by writing DODownloadMode = 0 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization. The local Microsoft DeliveryOptimization ADMX and ADML files define value 0 as HTTP only with no peering. The app previously wrote the same value under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\DeliveryOptimization; it now uses the documented policy path.

setting.name: Delivery Optimization Download Mode
setting.casual_explanation: Delivery Optimization controls whether Windows uses other PCs to help download updates and Microsoft content. This policy sets how much peer-to-peer sharing Delivery Optimization is allowed to use.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when they want to limit or remove peer sharing for updates and downloads.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Setting it to HTTP only disables peer sharing, but it can also remove bandwidth-sharing benefits on local networks.
setting.professional_notes: DeliveryOptimization.admx documents the official policy path as HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization with value name DODownloadMode. The local ADMX and ADML files define value 0 as HTTP only with no peering. The previously used PolicyManager default path was removed from the product implementation on 2026-03-13 so the tweak now targets the documented policy surface directly.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps the normal Delivery Optimization behavior without this policy override.

allowed_values.label: HTTP only
allowed_values.meaning: Delivery Optimization uses HTTP only and no peering.

allowed_values.label: LAN peering
allowed_values.meaning: Delivery Optimization uses HTTP blended with peering behind the same NAT.

allowed_values.label: Group peering
allowed_values.meaning: Delivery Optimization uses HTTP blended with peering across a private group.

allowed_values.label: Internet peering
allowed_values.meaning: Delivery Optimization uses HTTP blended with Internet peering.

allowed_values.label: Simple
allowed_values.meaning: HTTP only, no peering, and no use of the Delivery Optimization cloud service.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now writes the official HTTP-only value 0 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization\DODownloadMode.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems supporting the Delivery Optimization policy
windows_defaults.notes: This record validates the official policy surface.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
avoid_for: Environments that explicitly want no Delivery Optimization peering
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want normal Windows Delivery Optimization behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need an explicit no-peering policy.
tradeoffs: Windows keeps its default Delivery Optimization behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: HTTP only (no peering)
intended_for: Environments that do not want update peering
avoid_for: Networks that intentionally use local or group peering to reduce bandwidth

states.rationale: Use the official HTTP-only value with no peering.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Delivery Optimization downloads without peer sharing.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want LAN or group peering benefits.
tradeoffs: Bandwidth-saving benefits from peering are removed.
recommended_profiles.notes: Validated official policy path and value.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DeliveryOptimization.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps DODownloadMode under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization and defines enum values 0, 1, 2, 3, 99, and 100.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DeliveryOptimization.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains that value 0 means HTTP only with no peering, and documents the other Delivery Optimization modes.

decision.why: The official Delivery Optimization policy path and enum values are clear from the local Microsoft ADMX and ADML files, and the app now writes the documented policy surface directly.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: DeliveryOptimization.admx: `valueName="DODownloadMode"`; DeliveryOptimization.adml: `Download Mode` / `Specifies the method that Delivery Optimization can use to download content on behalf of various Microsoft products.` The local ADMX/ADML pair documents DODownloadMode under the official policy path and its enum values 0, 1, 2, 3, 99, and 100.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX and ADML files document the exact Delivery Optimization policy surface under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization, and the app now writes that documented path.
